title: Driver & Capability System

classes.name: DriverService
methods: call_new_({actor, service, service_name, iface, method, args, _skip_usage}): Promise<Object>
classes.notes: Central dispatcher. call() wraps _call() with error handling. _call() resolves aliases, finds service, builds context, delegates to call_new_(). call_new_() checks permissions (PermissionUtil.join service:name:ii:iface), resolves effective policy, builds Invoker with 3 decorators: rate-limit, add-metadata, result-coercion.

classes.name: DriverUsagePolicyService
classes.notes: Handles permission scan → policy resolution flow. get_effective_policy scans permission for 'service:name:ii:trait', calls select_best_option_, get_policies_for_option_, then interprets policy via svc_su.sudo + svc_systemData.interpret. Duplicates logic from DriverService.call_new_ (noted as TODO: DRY).

classes.name: CoercionService
classes.notes: coerce() uses TypeSpec.adapt() + hash() for caching, filters coercions by target type, recursively resolves intermediate types. Caches results in typed_value.calculated_coercions_[target_hash].

classes.name: DriverError
classes.notes: Plain error class (not extending Error). Serializable alongside APIError. Used in _driver_response_from_error to produce {success:false, error: serialized}.

classes.name: FileFacade
classes.notes: Lazy-evaluated file representation. Avoids unnecessary S3 downloads when driver can accept S3 bucket info directly. Used as argument type for file parameters in driver calls.

classes.name: TypedValue
classes.notes: Runtime wrapper for typed driver results/args. Coercion cache keyed by TypeSpec hash.

classes.name: TypeSpec
classes.notes: Type descriptor for driver values. Used by CoercionService to match produces/consumes.

classes.name: BaseService
classes.notes: Common base for all services. Provides this.services, this.log, this.errors.

classes.name: Invoker
classes.notes: Decorator chain runner. Used in DriverService.call_new_() with decorators: rate-limit, add-metadata, result-coercion.

key_relationships: DriverService depends_on CoercionService (svc_coercion.coerce in result-coercion decorator)
DriverService depends_on APIError (permission forbidden, arg errors)
DriverService uses TypedValue (instanceof check for result coercion)
DriverService uses TypeSpec (via CoercionService, interface registry lookup)
DriverService uses Invoker (decorator pattern in call_new_)
CoercionService uses TypedValue (coerce input/output, caches in calculated_coercions_)
FileFacade uses MultiValue (lazy factory-based value resolution)
FileFacade used_by DriverService (as file argument type in _process_args via types.js)
driver_dispatch_flow: _call: resolve driver alias (iface_to_driver table, service_aliases, interface_service_aliases)
_call: _process_args(iface, method, args) — validate & consolidate via type registry
_call: get_service_or_throw_(driver, iface) — lookup service or dynamic class from drivers registry
_call → call_new_({actor, service, service_name, iface, method, args})
call_new_: Invoker.create([rate-limit decorator, add-metadata decorator, result-coercion decorator])
result-coercion decorator: if result instanceof TypedValue → svc_coercion.coerce(desired_type, result)
error_handling: APIError and DriverError are serializable, others emit error report
Success shape: {success: true, service: {name, version?}, result, metadata}
important_notes: DriverService is itself a driver (IMPLEMENTS driver interface) for usage queries. iface_to_driver table maps legacy interface names to driver names. Test mode (Context.get('test_mode')) can skip usage tracking. Rate-limiting applied per-policy-holder via svc_rateLimit. Policy data interpreted via svc_systemData.interpret in svc_su.sudo context.
